==> Caveats
For the system Java wrappers to find this JDK, symlink it with
  sudo ln -sfn /opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/libexec/openjdk.jdk /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk-21.jdk

openjdk@21 is keg-only, which means it was not symlinked into /opt/homebrew,
because this is an alternate version of another formula.

If you need to have openjdk@21 first in your PATH, run:
  echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc

For compilers to find openjdk@21 you may need to set:
  export CPPFLAGS="-I/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/include"

==> Caveats
==> openjdk@21
For the system Java wrappers to find this JDK, symlink it with
  sudo ln -sfn /opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/libexec/openjdk.jdk /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/openjdk-21.jdk

openjdk@21 is keg-only, which means it was not symlinked into /opt/homebrew,
because this is an alternate version of another formula.

If you need to have openjdk@21 first in your PATH, run:
  echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc

For compilers to find openjdk@21 you may need to set:
  export CPPFLAGS="-I/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/include"

        echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] Token refresh completed successfully" >> "$REFRESH_LOG"; failure_count=0;
    else
        failure_count=$((failure_count + 1)); echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] Token refresh FAILED (failure $failure_count/$MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES)" >> "$REFRESH_LOG"; if [[ $failure_count -ge $MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES ]]; then
            echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] Token refresh exceeded $MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES consecutive failures, stopping background refresh" >> "$REFRESH_LOG"; break;
        fi;
    fi;
done )  (wd: ~)
(wd now: ~/librdkafka)
